channel.title: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers" - Have you joined the site because you want to write an SCP article or tale of your own, sometime now or in the future? Post your introduction here!

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Hello there,
The SCP foundation first caught my attention when i saw a video about it on yt. Ive been reading through the SCP files a bit, and i finally decided i wanna join and create my own SCPs. I am already flowing over with ideas, and have already started working on a file.

Personally i think kebab is best food, as its is made fast, and if made good, is the perfekt balance between meat, salad and bread.

Anyways, im looking forward to being part of this community

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Hello everyone, I am Dr. Nightowl

I have been reading SCPs and watching the videos for a few months now, and think it would fun and interesting to write an SCP myself. I'm not that great of a writer so I am gonna try putting my best foot forward and write some SCPs

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Hello hello Gruvint8! Welcome to the Wiki! I'm Ralliston, a site author and a member of the Community Outreach Team - with this helpful little comment, I'll try to help you find your way around the site and link a bunch of useful places and links that will help you in your SCP journey.

It's awesome to see someone with prior writing experience joining the wiki - I'm sure what you'll come up with during your stay here will be absolutely amazing. I can't wait to see your stuff. It's also great to see that after all of these years "lurking", you finally decided to make an account on-site. You'd be surprised at how many people stay only as lurkers and never properly become members. After all, now you can actively affect the quality of articles posted on-site by voting, you can comment, and, most importantly it seems, contribute.

Without further ado though, let's click this collapsible, shall we? :)

+ show block
– hide block

If you're going to try your hand at writing, try out our Sandbox. You can make yourself a page here to store your drafts, which are in-works articles. Using it, you can link the drafts to reviewers to get their opinions on them before posting. It really helps everyone out a lot.

For brainstorming concepts, try out the Ideas and Brainstorming forum. You can run your ideas by experienced members of the community if you need help refining and fleshing out your concept. It's a very helpful little place for when you want to start your writing journey, especially when you're not sure if the idea is all that great.

If you have a draft ready, make a thread in the Drafts and Critiques forum. Remember to check the stickied posts in the relevant thread for guidelines on how best to use each forum. You can also try the IRC chat for real-time feedback.

From site usage to writing tips, the Guide Hub contains a wealth of resources geared towards helping members produce successful articles. Within the Guides, you will find useful information written by experienced members of the site for the use of newer ones.

The more time you take to familiarize yourself with the SCP style, the drafting process, and the community, the greater your chances are for success! Remember - there is no rush to writing - take your time! The more time you spend on fleshing your ideas out, the more your chance of success becomes!

As far as reading is concerned, you might want to check out Top Rated New Pages, which will display the most popular recent articles. It's helpful to keep up with what's being received well by the community to implement into your very own writing. Alternatively, you might want to look at the User Curated Lists page, where members of the community list and rank their favorite articles in different genres and themes, showing which attempts at such are successful and what the community likes.

Best of luck with your future contributions to the site! I hope you enjoy your time here as much as we do! If you have any more questions, feel free to message me here on Wikidot or on the IRC chats!

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Good day everyone,

I'm a long time fan of SCP and have read my fair share of articles through the years. But since the start of corona I started getting into writing. I had allot of SCP ideas but was never sure about sharing them. Then in this past week, an idea of an SCP came to mind and I wrote it down. And after letting my close friends read it I was getting more comfortable with sharing my writing and finally decided to join. I hope to get better at writing through here and to just have an amazing time in this lovely community!

Regards,
TurtwigTheSpy (Some random Dutch Lad)

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Hi everybody, my names Phil and I'm just here chasing my joy.

I'm a recent University graduate who after some self evaluating recognized that the aspect of my degree I enjoyed most was the storytelling. I've always been a fan of the SCP universe and the way its many stories are spun together into an ever expanding universe of many minds. So I thought, where better to practice writing than here?

I'm a massive fan of collaborative story telling, a result of my immersion in ttrpg culture and nerdom, and really wanted the experience of discussing narrative with a community. This will be some of my first writing uploaded to a large public space and I find myself both excited and nervous to take the plunge. Thanks so much for anytime you might've spent on my ramblings and I hope to talk to and share my work with people soon. :)

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Man it's been a while since I've written any kind of introduction post.
Hi- I'm Leah. I originally just joined this Wiki because I wanted to read the SCP-4231 article and it is (rightfully so) hidden behind content warnings. But I've had a character idea for a while and so I started working on a tale surrounding her not long ago. Hopefully I'll become happy enough with it that I'll risk baring my creative soul to a bunch of strangers on a forum, which is an absolutely terrifying concept and I mean that with no sarcasm because I'm an anxious mess :)

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Greetings everyone! I am a beginner at writing so I decided to write SCP because:
1- I love the SCP lore
2- I have an ambition to be a renown writer
Anyway i look foward on working with you guys and knowing you all! I will already start working!

P.S- I'm Brazilian. Come to brazil please?

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Hey, I’m Zane, and I’ve been reading for several years, but only recently realized I could join the site. I don’t know what else to put here, except I want to write about a diner that if you eat the cheesecake, you get sent to another dimension. I also want to try to get into the 914 test logs. See you on the other side,
-Z

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Hi, this is my introduction as a hopeful writer of future SCPs and tales. I have been writing for a long time, and have been a follower of the SCP foundation for a long time, although I don't have an exact length of time. I'm not entirely sure what more information I should put into the intro, so here goes :)

Gruvint8

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded: Greeting everyone my name is classified of course but PLEASE call me tukapee or tuk for short, overtime I may give out my real name, unless thats a requirement, anyway the best food to eat with friends are berries! Cause their juicy, healthy, and full of flavor! I hope I can be agreat member to the community and have my scp on the list with the othera^^

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded: Hey all, I've been reading up on a ton of SCPs recently, been aware of the community for a few years now. Figured I'd try my hand at writing one. If it goes well, I might try writing more in the near future if inspiration strikes me. I'm still waiting to join the sandbox wiki, but if anyone can point me in the direction here on the main site where I can get feedback on the SCP I'm writing I would greatly appreciate it.

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Hello!

I really enjoy writing, and decided to try out writing my own SCP. It's going to take some work, as I want my first SCP to not fail (my idea is centered around an anomalous nuclear power plant of unknown origin in northen Quebec). I also decided to drop in here, as it seems like it's rude to pop in to a community and not get to know anybody.

I mean, looking at the other forums, everybody seems pretty nice, so that makes me want to be a part of that community.

Anyways, not sure how I came off there. Oh well.

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Hello hello DrTarin! Welcome to the Wiki! I'm Ralliston, a site author and a member of the Community Outreach Team - with this helpful little comment, I'll try to help you find your way around the site and link a bunch of useful places and links that will help you in your SCP journey.

It's great to see that after all of these years, you finally decided to make an account on-site. After all, that's the only way you can contribute - speaking of which, it's also awesome to see you want to be a site author too! Don't worry, I'm sure that during your stay here you definitely will gain some knowledge regarding how to write things the site likes and how to write stuff you are happy with, too!

Without further ado though, let's click this collapsible, shall we? :)

+ show block
– hide block

If you're going to try your hand at writing, try out our Sandbox. You can make yourself a page here to store your drafts, which are in-works articles. Using it, you can link the drafts to reviewers to get their opinions on them before posting. It really helps everyone out a lot.

For brainstorming concepts, try out the Ideas and Brainstorming forum. You can run your ideas by experienced members of the community if you need help refining and fleshing out your concept. It's a very helpful little place for when you want to start your writing journey, especially when you're not sure if the idea is all that great.

If you have a draft ready, make a thread in the Drafts and Critiques forum. Remember to check the stickied posts in the relevant thread for guidelines on how best to use each forum. You can also try the IRC chat for real-time feedback.

From site usage to writing tips, the Guide Hub contains a wealth of resources geared towards helping members produce successful articles. Within the Guides, you will find useful information written by experienced members of the site for the use of newer ones.

The more time you take to familiarize yourself with the SCP style, the drafting process, and the community, the greater your chances are for success! Remember - there is no rush to writing - take your time! The more time you spend on fleshing your ideas out, the more your chance of success becomes!

As far as reading is concerned, you might want to check out Top Rated New Pages, which will display the most popular recent articles. It's helpful to keep up with what's being received well by the community to implement into your very own writing. Alternatively, you might want to look at the User Curated Lists page, where members of the community list and rank their favorite articles in different genres and themes, showing which attempts at such are successful and what the community likes.

Best of luck with your future contributions to the site! I hope you enjoy your time here as much as we do! If you have any more questions, feel free to message me here on Wikidot or on the IRC chats!

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Hello everyone!

I´ve had my head wrapped around SCPs a year ago, and I always wanted to write my own SCPs. Although I am not a pro at writing I still love to create SCPs. I don't mind any criticism as long as it helps me to improve on my writing.

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Hello there, my name is Angelo, it is a pleasure to be here.

I'm quite an old SCP fan, but only now I've decided to try and get closer to its fans and, in the near future, I will also take a crack at writing my own anomaly. I hope we can get along just fine!

About this month's question, I find it nice to have ice cream and, maybe, pizza with other people.

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Hello hello baydog! Welcome to the Wiki! I'm Ralliston, a site author and a member of the Community Outreach Team - with this helpful little comment, I'll try to help you find your way around the site and link a bunch of useful places and links that will help you in your SCP journey.

It's great to see that after all of these years, you finally decided to make an account on-site. You'd be surprised at how many people remain lurkers forever - it's a real shame, considering that becoming ~one of us~ can not only let you vote, which also makes you able to affect the quality of works posted onsite, but also lets you comment and contribute. Speaking of which - it's great to see you want to do that. I can't wait to see what you'll come up with!

Without further ado though, let's click this collapsible, shall we? :)

+ show block
– hide block

If you're going to try your hand at writing, try out our Sandbox. You can make yourself a page here to store your drafts, which are in-works articles. Using it, you can link the drafts to reviewers to get their opinions on them before posting. It really helps everyone out a lot.

For brainstorming concepts, try out the Ideas and Brainstorming forum. You can run your ideas by experienced members of the community if you need help refining and fleshing out your concept. It's a very helpful little place for when you want to start your writing journey, especially when you're not sure if the idea is all that great.

If you have a draft ready, make a thread in the Drafts and Critiques forum. Remember to check the stickied posts in the relevant thread for guidelines on how best to use each forum. You can also try the IRC chat for real-time feedback.

From site usage to writing tips, the Guide Hub contains a wealth of resources geared towards helping members produce successful articles. Within the Guides, you will find useful information written by experienced members of the site for the use of newer ones.

The more time you take to familiarize yourself with the SCP style, the drafting process, and the community, the greater your chances are for success! Remember - there is no rush to writing - take your time! The more time you spend on fleshing your ideas out, the more your chance of success becomes!

As far as reading is concerned, you might want to check out Top Rated New Pages, which will display the most popular recent articles. It's helpful to keep up with what's being received well by the community to implement into your very own writing. Alternatively, you might want to look at the User Curated Lists page, where members of the community list and rank their favorite articles in different genres and themes, showing which attempts at such are successful and what the community likes.

Best of luck with your future contributions to the site! I hope you enjoy your time here as much as we do! If you have any more questions, feel free to message me here on Wikidot or on the IRC chats!

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Hii! I've always known about SCP but my friend got me more into it and now it's been a big interest of mine for quite a while now!! I've been sitting on ideas and such for some time but I finally decided to get right down to it and join the site haha

I'm kind of nervous about it but hopefully people will like the things I come up with! I also do art :-)

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Hi there,

according to my internet history I was aware of the SCP-Foundation since the summer of 2019. It came to my attention due to me looking for material to use in a Pen and Paper, afterwards I went deeper while watching videos about the Containment Breach videos and since that time I read some entries here. Also I recently started with reading tales from the Canon Hub.

I caught myself having thoughts about writing SCPs of my own and have some ideas there, which is why I decided to finally join. With that I could maybe start voting and share my opinion

One of the earliest SCPs I have read and think about now and then would be the soup bowl SCP-348. Another I like to think about would be red ice SCP-009.

The best kind of food to eat with friends would be the one you make together. You can then see preferences of your friends in there and try something out for yourself as well. Also cooking together is more fun.

I hope I can make some of your time more entertaining.

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Hey guys!

I've known about SCP for years, but only recently did I start to really get into it (for once, the youtube algorithm worked out in my favor). Normally, I write about modern issues or fantasy worlds, but I decided to try my hand at this. I love all forms of animation, and I love psychology. I have a few ideas on what I want to write about already, so I hope everyone enjoys.

Thanks for accepting me, I really want to contribute!

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Hello, and welcome ObtuseGryphon! Trust me, you are not alone in taking your time to write your own SCP, as many of us have waited a long time before thinking about writing our own. It's all about improvement here too, everyone is out to help make your idea the best it can be. And, no matter what the part you struggle on is there is someone that specializes in that department to help you out. You'll do just fine around here. Make sure to take your time and get acquainted with how everything here works, and make sure to check out IRC chat too!

For my recommendation, you should check out SCP-5109

+ Resources
- Hide Resources

For posting ideas, make sure you go through the proper greenlighting process on the idea forums first. Remember that there is no rush, and you should take time familiarizing yourself with how Series 6 articles are formatted. We are a rather unique bunch so I'm sure just reading and being a part of the community will be rather fun for you!

If you're going to try your hand at writing, try out our sandbox. You can make yourself a page here to store your drafts.

Sandbox

For hashing out concepts, try out the Ideas and Brainstorming forum. You can run your ideas by the community if you need help refining your concept.

If you have a draft ready, make a thread in the Drafts and Critiques forum. Remember to check the stickied posts in the relevant thread for guidelines on how best to use each forum. You can also try the IRC chat for real-time feedback.

From site usage to writing tips, the Guide Hub contains a wealth of resources geared towards helping members produce successful articles.

The more time you take to familiarize yourself with the SCP style, the drafting process, and the community, the greater your chances are for success!

As far as reading is concerned, you might want to check out our Top Rated New Pages, which will display the most popular, recent articles. It's helpful to keep up with what's being received well by the community.


Best of luck!

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Greetings Neccy, welcome! Fun is pretty much the goal here, this place is a labor of love after all. So trust me, enjoy away. Sharing things is what we do around here, and sometimes the ideas don't make it past the conceptualization phase of development. However even having just one other person think your idea is neat is definitely enough for me. So trust me, you'll fit in just fine. Make sure to take your time and get acquainted with how everything here works, and make sure to check out IRC chat too!

For my recommendation, you should check out SCP-4333

+ Resources
- Hide Resources

For posting ideas, make sure you go through the proper greenlighting process on the idea forums first. Remember that there is no rush, and you should take time familiarizing yourself with how Series 6 articles are formatted. We are a rather unique bunch so I'm sure just reading and being a part of the community will be rather fun for you!

If you're going to try your hand at writing, try out our sandbox. You can make yourself a page here to store your drafts.

Sandbox

For hashing out concepts, try out the Ideas and Brainstorming forum. You can run your ideas by the community if you need help refining your concept.

If you have a draft ready, make a thread in the Drafts and Critiques forum. Remember to check the stickied posts in the relevant thread for guidelines on how best to use each forum. You can also try the IRC chat for real-time feedback.

From site usage to writing tips, the Guide Hub contains a wealth of resources geared towards helping members produce successful articles.

The more time you take to familiarize yourself with the SCP style, the drafting process, and the community, the greater your chances are for success!

As far as reading is concerned, you might want to check out our Top Rated New Pages, which will display the most popular, recent articles. It's helpful to keep up with what's being received well by the community.


Best of luck!

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Hey Riruu, and welcome! No need to worry about readers here, there are always plenty of people around to read and review your works. Language can be a tricky thing, but most people on here are understanding of many difficulties there may be so not to worry about that either. I always say to take your time, and I can never stress that enough for anyone. There isn't a rush, or a timer on posting so take as long as you need to get acquainted with the site and comfortable with your idea. Make sure to check out IRC chat too!

For my recommendation, you should check out SCP-5514

+ Resources
- Hide Resources

For posting ideas, make sure you go through the proper greenlighting process on the idea forums first. Remember that there is no rush, and you should take time familiarizing yourself with how Series 6 articles are formatted. We are a rather unique bunch so I'm sure just reading and being a part of the community will be rather fun for you!

If you're going to try your hand at writing, try out our sandbox. You can make yourself a page here to store your drafts.

Sandbox

For hashing out concepts, try out the Ideas and Brainstorming forum. You can run your ideas by the community if you need help refining your concept.

If you have a draft ready, make a thread in the Drafts and Critiques forum. Remember to check the stickied posts in the relevant thread for guidelines on how best to use each forum. You can also try the IRC chat for real-time feedback.

From site usage to writing tips, the Guide Hub contains a wealth of resources geared towards helping members produce successful articles.

The more time you take to familiarize yourself with the SCP style, the drafting process, and the community, the greater your chances are for success!

As far as reading is concerned, you might want to check out our Top Rated New Pages, which will display the most popular, recent articles. It's helpful to keep up with what's being received well by the community.


Best of luck!

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
My dearest gentlemen, ferals, and domesticated cryptids, I bid you a good current concept of time.

I actually don't remember how I found SCP. I think I saw a random video on youtube on an SCP, then found the Oversimplified SCP comic series. After that, it was down the rabbit hole for me.

Currently, I just want to write my own SCP article, though I have a feeling I might write a few SCP tales later on. What I have in mind may not be original, but hey maybe I can do it better than anyone else. I doubt that though, there's a lot of great writers here already.

Honestly, in the way of hobbies, I do a little bit of everything. Though I always have had a major fascination with space, other dimensions, and generally cool unknown mysterious places.

My only real worry coming into this site is that my stuff isn't going to be good enough. While I have ideas I feel like they might not be unique or original enough to get any attention, or that I might not be able to bring out the full potential of the idea.

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Hello

My name is ElPotato and I have been lurking on this site for a year or so and decided to join and write my own SCP. Please do correct and spelling/grammar mistakes. Thank you and have a good day!

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
I've read meny SCPs for the year and a half I've been here. For the past few months I have written SCPs on paper over and over befor I joined the site. Only now I have decided to share my SCP ideas to the world

If I make mistakes, you are welcome to correct me. I am always trying to improve my writing skills and get better with time. Thank you for accepting me in this amazing community!

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Hi, I'm Impperatrix, but you can call me Impp.

I'm a long time fan of SCPs and the SCP community, but as english is not my native language, I've always been very shy to participate. Feel free to correct my grammar!

I'm also a RPG player, Sailor Moon fan, cinephile (but I'm not obnoxious about this, I promise!) and totally a dog person (but I like cats too ;D)

If you're confused, my pronouns are he/his and I'm openly homossexual!

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Hi, I'm Celare!

I'm a writer from the US, and I have a passion for all conceptual science fiction. As you can imagine, this site is a wonderland for me! I've been lurking for quite a while now, even browsing the forums, but I'm very excited to finally begin contributing! I'd have to say that my favorite skip is… 5494. Masterful.

My only question is this: new contributors seem to frequently have some difficulty with the greenlight process. What's the best way to make sure I'm not bothering staff when I approach them for critique?

I'll see you all out there!

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Hi all!

I've been a long-time lurker of the site and have always been super interested in the universe and love the genre.

I decided to join the site yesterday and am excited to finally contribute and hear feedback. I don't have the most writing experience but I enjoy it regardless and have some ideas I would love to write down and share with you guys.

You all are super talented and I'm glad to finally be exercising my literary muscles on something I enjoy.

Cheers!

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Hello there, Offlite!

Happy New Year to you too, and welcome to the SCP Wiki. It's great to see after a few years of reading around, you've decided to join the site. While it can be a bit difficult starting out, as long as you check out the guides and such, knowing the technical side and guidelines regarding contributing to the site is a piece of cake. When getting into writing for the Wiki, it's a good idea to read some of the well-liked newer articles to get a feel for what types of articles people are interested in as of now. I personally recommend reading SCP-5509. Anyway, here are some resources to help you out around the site:

◎ Open Resources ◎
◉ Close Resources ◉

If you're going to try your hand at writing, try out our sandbox. You can make yourself a page here to store your drafts.

Sandbox

For hashing out concepts, try out the Ideas and Brainstorming forum. You can run your ideas by the community if you need help refining your concept.

If you have a draft ready, make a thread in the Drafts and Critiques forum. Remember to check the stickied posts in the relevant thread for guidelines on how best to use each forum. You can also try the IRC chat for real-time feedback.

From site usage to writing tips, the Guide Hub contains a wealth of resources geared towards helping members produce successful articles.

The more time you take to familiarize yourself with the SCP style, the drafting process, and the community, the greater your chances are for success!

As far as reading is concerned, you might want to check out our Top Rated New Pages, which will display the most popular, recent articles. It's helpful to keep up with what's being received well by the community.

With that said, I hope you have an amazing time here.

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded: Hello everyone, I made the account a year ago but completely forgot about it. I'm not really a writer but I will try my best to follow the site rules and try to create some hopefully interesting pages. As for food, pizza is definitely at the top unless your friend orders a pineapple pizza, in which case the friendship would cease to exist.

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Greetings.

I've been a fan of the SCPverse for years, so I'm pretty familiar with the whole thing by this point. My first contact with it occurred when I was like 14. I was pretty obsessed with creepypastas at the time, and I downloaded an app about it which happened to contain a bunch of SCP articles (mainly series I). I read through most of it, and discovered the wiki shortly afterwards. I tried to get into it then, but my lack of maturity and a low english level prevented me from actually understanding what I was looking at. But I kept coming back to it, each time understanding a bit more of what I was reading.

I've been flirting with the idea of writing something for the site for months now, so I decided to do something with my life and join the wiki already. I will submit something in the near future, probably.

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Hello everyone! It’s quite an honor (to me at least xD) to finally be apart of the creative minds of SCP. Been a fan of SCP for approximately 5-6 years now, back when I thought they were real and got myself scared. Anyways I hope to learn everything there is to this site and to meet the admins and mods.

But now to properly introduce myself, my name is Ethan and I live in Australia but New-Zealand born. I love to game in my free time (cliche I know) but I also love watching movies, especially marvel movies. I am currently fresh out of high school and on my way to study computer science at uni.

My main endeavor joining this creative writing group was to maybe practice my creative writing skills and ultimately craft my very own SCP. However I am also quite intrigued by the SCP fandom/lore and do wish to learn more about that as well.

Lastly I may or may not need help navigating around this site cause my brain is fried. I think that is all, stay safe, keep writing and I hope to see you around.
Thank-you.

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Hello everyone

Long story short, I joined the site by watching some scp videos and googled the foundation to read the original tales by myself. There are a bunch of great stories here and I want to write too, as well as read a lot more.

Enough talking. Happy new year!

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded: I used to write a lot of stuff, but then I broke a mirror and had to go away for 7 years.

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded: Staff only delete posts that contain severe rulebreaking material. Just edit over repeat/extra posts with "ignore, wrong place" or "ignore, repeat" or the like, and double-check the location next time. Not a big deal unless you're spamming. :)

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Greetings.

The Foundation is one of the internet rabbit holes I've fallen down every now and then since probably 2014. I recently read an SCP that was a guy who kept coming up with science fiction ideas but couldn't commit to actually fleshing one out, and honestly I can super relate because I've always had great ideas that I couldn't put to paper. I figured this site would work better for me than trying to write a whole novel, so I finally joined and I'm happy to be here. I look forward to getting to know other writers and sharing my ideas, if they're any good.

Happy New Year!

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Hello, fellow writers!

As of now, I'm still an amateur writer but with the help of the reviewers, I'll be able to create an SCP worth remembering. My one problem is I have no idea where to begin. If anyone who sees this post can think of an SCP that can get my creative juices flowing, please, send the links my way. It's a privilege to finally be a member of this family.

If you're also interested, I'm also a relatively decent person to bounce ideas off of so if you're also running dry of ideas right now, then I'm your man.

Thanks again and happy writing ;)

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded: My bad, can someone delete this, didn't realise new post button was at the bottom of the screen xD

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Hey Everyone,

Happy New Year!

Thanks for letting me become a part of this impressive community. I can’t wait to create some content to add my ideas to the thousands already on here. Really enjoying diving through the site finding all the unique ideas.

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Hey Researcher Bevan and welcome to the site. No matter your method of learning about SCPs, you're definitely welcome to the site. New ideas are always great, and your enthusiasm will be appreciated! Make sure to take your time and get acquainted with how everything here works, and make sure to check out IRC chat too!

For my recommendation, you should check out [[|SCP-5707]

+ Resources
- Hide Resources

For posting ideas, make sure you go through the proper greenlighting process on the idea forums first. Remember that there is no rush, and you should take time familiarizing yourself with how Series 6 articles are formatted. We are a rather unique bunch so I'm sure just reading and being a part of the community will be rather fun for you!

If you're going to try your hand at writing, try out our sandbox. You can make yourself a page here to store your drafts.

Sandbox

For hashing out concepts, try out the Ideas and Brainstorming forum. You can run your ideas by the community if you need help refining your concept.

If you have a draft ready, make a thread in the Drafts and Critiques forum. Remember to check the stickied posts in the relevant thread for guidelines on how best to use each forum. You can also try the IRC chat for real-time feedback.

From site usage to writing tips, the Guide Hub contains a wealth of resources geared towards helping members produce successful articles.

The more time you take to familiarize yourself with the SCP style, the drafting process, and the community, the greater your chances are for success!

As far as reading is concerned, you might want to check out our Top Rated New Pages, which will display the most popular, recent articles. It's helpful to keep up with what's being received well by the community.


Best of luck!

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Greetings Zamarak500, and welcome to the new year of the site. Being a history buff is both awesome(fellow history buff) and helpful since historical knowledge can always help bolster a good article. Also Roman history is just neat. And well maybe you have been here before, but we made you forget? Who knows? Make sure to take your time and get acquainted with how everything here works, and make sure to check out IRC chat too!

For my recommendation, you should check out SCP-5700

+ Resources
- Hide Resources

For posting ideas, make sure you go through the proper greenlighting process on the idea forums first. Remember that there is no rush, and you should take time familiarizing yourself with how Series 6 articles are formatted. We are a rather unique bunch so I'm sure just reading and being a part of the community will be rather fun for you!

If you're going to try your hand at writing, try out our sandbox. You can make yourself a page here to store your drafts.

Sandbox

For hashing out concepts, try out the Ideas and Brainstorming forum. You can run your ideas by the community if you need help refining your concept.

If you have a draft ready, make a thread in the Drafts and Critiques forum. Remember to check the stickied posts in the relevant thread for guidelines on how best to use each forum. You can also try the IRC chat for real-time feedback.

From site usage to writing tips, the Guide Hub contains a wealth of resources geared towards helping members produce successful articles.

The more time you take to familiarize yourself with the SCP style, the drafting process, and the community, the greater your chances are for success!

As far as reading is concerned, you might want to check out our Top Rated New Pages, which will display the most popular, recent articles. It's helpful to keep up with what's being received well by the community.


Best of luck!

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Hello, and Happy New Year. I am SOYA-001, a site member of SCP-JP / CN.

Although SCP-JP has written several highly rated articles, I participated in SCP-EN mainly to vote on articles. I'm not very good at English, so I may often rely on machine translation for conversation.

Nice to meet you everyone.

PS: I enjoy eating conveyor belt sushi with my friends.

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Hello, I am me.

I consider myself fairly new to SCP in general, though I've now read probably a couple hundred articles/tales and lurked a fair bit on-site—fell in love with everything about it (093 is the one that hooked me para siempre).

As a writer, I'm…competent. I hope to use that to ultimately contribute here. For now, I'm absorbing all I can.

I like music (listening and making) and video games (mostly RPGs) and I collect tarantulas. I'm very excited to finally be a member of this community and I apologize for any grievous newb transgressions I may have made thus far.

Thanks.

Random question: Friends? W-what's that? Is it tasty??

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Howdy everyone!

I've read SCPs for the past couple of years, but only recently have I decided to try my hand at writing them. I'm probably not the best writer (not by a long shot), so I look forward to any constructive criticism that will help me improve!

Feel free to correct my grammar if it's absolutely horrendous; my knowledge of grammar mostly stems from Weird Al's Word Crimes, so perhaps writing here can help to develop my grammatical skills.

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Hello! I'm Neccy,

I'm a simple person who has great interest in character creation. My writing skills aren't very advanced at all but I find writing fun never the less.

I've always been very curious about the SCP foundation and its vast community and I thought that I might as well have a crack at creating some SCPs. I'm not expecting to become a popular writer or anything, I'm just excited to share my ideas with others who have a similar interest and possibly have one of my SCPs be liked by some people.

I'm not a very interesting person so I don't think there is much else to say.

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded: Psst… there's at least one person every month who doesn't read the blue text in the top post! Please read the blue text!

item.title: Junior Staff Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: braxiusLT, please note that when creating a post in this, or really any on-site forum, you should click "New Post" at the bottom of the thread rather than clicking "Reply" on someone else's post. Doing the latter will cause the comment to nest wrong as can be seen here. Thank you.

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Good day!

First, as an amateur writer i wanted to try SCP. None of my stories were read by anyone, so it may be fun to finally get some usefull critics.

Second, english is not my main language, so i may have some spelling errors sometimes. I'll do my best for not making them.

Third and final, i am socialy awkward, but i still make too many questions. From today i start my first scp writing, who knows how long it'll take me to finish, i'll be counting on you all.

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Good day!

First, as an amateur writer i wanted to try SCP. None of my stories were read by anyone, so it may be fun to finally get some usefull critics.

Second, english is not my main language, so i may have some spelling errors sometimes. I'll do my best for not making them.

Third and final, i am socialy awkward, but i still make too many questions. From today i start my first scp writing, who knows how long it'll take me to finish, i'll be counting on you all.

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded: Hello everyone, after listening few of the SCP articles in youtube and especially after SCP-5000, I got inspired to have my crack at writing again. SCP format is very appealing to me, as a software developer I'm used to writing documentation type of articles. Hoping to get greenlit soon.

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded: Happy new years! I've read and listened to SCP stuff for a few years now, and I've gotten into writing and it's the career I'd like to have. I'm still in high school, I've taken a few classes like English 101, Ap Lang, and Creative writing, but I'd love to expand my writing style to an even more developed manner. The SCP community has always had my interest since I read my first article and I wanted to contribute, but I was 14. I'm really excited to let my creativity to flow.

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded: Happy new year everyone! I guess I'm going under the moniker "Offlite" these days. I just joined a few days ago, but have been reading/listening to SCP content for a couple years now. I didn't realize how possible it was to contribute before, and now that I have a chance to I'm pretty stoked. I hope I can bring something worthwhile to the site. Thanks for having me!

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Ahoy there, wantsa5dayweekend!

Welcome to the SCP Wiki, and I look forward to seeing what ideas you have in mind. Seeing that you're new to the community and just getting into writing, I strongly suggest you check out some of the newer articles such as SCP-4661 to gauge the currently enjoyed style of writing. Additionally, here are some other resources you should check out which will help you a ton:

◎ Open Resources ◎
◉ Close Resources ◉

If you're going to try your hand at writing, try out our sandbox. You can make yourself a page here to store your drafts.

Sandbox

For hashing out concepts, try out the Ideas and Brainstorming forum. You can run your ideas by the community if you need help refining your concept.

If you have a draft ready, make a thread in the Drafts and Critiques forum. Remember to check the stickied posts in the relevant thread for guidelines on how best to use each forum. You can also try the IRC chat for real-time feedback.

From site usage to writing tips, the Guide Hub contains a wealth of resources geared towards helping members produce successful articles.

The more time you take to familiarize yourself with the SCP style, the drafting process, and the community, the greater your chances are for success!

As far as reading is concerned, you might want to check out our Top Rated New Pages, which will display the most popular, recent articles. It's helpful to keep up with what's being received well by the community.

I hope you have a great time here.

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Hello there, Evil Santa. Welcome to the SCP Wiki!

It's great to see after a few years, you've finally decided to actually join the site and contribute. If you don't have a particular concept/narrative in mind yet, that's okay. It's a good idea to read around the site first and check out some of the newer articles to get a better understanding in regards to what people are most interested in these days. I personally recommend checking out SCP-5038. Anyway, here are some resources to help you out around the site:

◎ Open Resources ◎
◉ Close Resources ◉

If you're going to try your hand at writing, try out our sandbox. You can make yourself a page here to store your drafts.

Sandbox

For hashing out concepts, try out the Ideas and Brainstorming forum. You can run your ideas by the community if you need help refining your concept.

If you have a draft ready, make a thread in the Drafts and Critiques forum. Remember to check the stickied posts in the relevant thread for guidelines on how best to use each forum. You can also try the IRC chat for real-time feedback.

From site usage to writing tips, the Guide Hub contains a wealth of resources geared towards helping members produce successful articles.

The more time you take to familiarize yourself with the SCP style, the drafting process, and the community, the greater your chances are for success!

As far as reading is concerned, you might want to check out our Top Rated New Pages, which will display the most popular, recent articles. It's helpful to keep up with what's being received well by the community.

With that said, I hope you enjoy your time here.

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Hello and Happy new year to all!

I've been listening to SCP podcasts a lot recently, and I thought I could use some idea swimming around in my brainspace to create something in the same style. I've listened to loads of SCP files and love the variety and creativity on display so I wanted to use some of my ideas. I'm really looking forward to exploring the site and seeing what crazy things people have come up with!

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Hi!

Actually a recent fan of the SCP universe (I've known it for years, but only recently got into it deep due to all these animated youtube videos explaining SCPs). Plus I decided that I should try to write one instead of moping that my ideas are too shit to be good for SCPs.

Other than SCP, I'm something of a history buff (especially Roman history), and I like comic books and manga.

So yeah, happy to be here and good year everyone!

For some weird reason, I have a lingering feeling I once did an intro on this site, but everything seems to indicate that it's my first time here. But I have this conviction that I've done an intro before! Should I call the SCP Foundation for that?

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Afternoon!

Im really glad to be accepted to this community and hope the smattering of ideas i have will help contribute.

I am a relatively new writer, always got ideas to expand on, however my actual editing skills need improving.

Cheers all!

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Hi. It is very exciting for me to finally be a member of this site. I've been a fan of the SCP universe for many years. I do not have an idea for a good SCP yet, but I am sure that sometime in the future I can think of something.

Thank you for accepting me. It really means a lot.

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded: Psst… there's at least one person every month who doesn't read the blue text in the top post! Please read the blue text!

item.title: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Note: If you're going to introduce yourself here, please scroll down and click "New Post" and NOT "reply", otherwise your comment will nest incorrectly!

Hello, prospective site writers! It's good to see you here!

This month's random question is: what do you think is the best kind of food to eat with friends?

First off, a couple standard things to keep in mind as you get started towards adding your voice to the Foundation universe:

  • Definitely read the How to Write an SCP Guide and the pages in the Guide Hub that are relevant to your ideas!
  • Make a sandbox page for your works in progress, reference notes, inspirational snippets, and more author things!
  • Get familiar with our three Writing Help Forums:
    • Questions Desk: For all your questions about the writing process!
    • Ideas and Brainstorming: For asking about concepts that you're thinking of writing up! This is an especially important step for first-time authors!
    • Drafts and Critiques: For reviews of your finished pieces, to see what edits need to be made to your work to make it ready for the mainlist!

If you're not sure what to talk about here, you can take a look at our How to Write an Intro/Welcome thread for ideas.

Feel free to respond to other new members if you find that your writing interests and/or plans overlap. The SCP wiki is a collaborative writing community, after all! You might even see some of the longtime writers here drop by with comments now and then. :)

Welcome to the site!
